prompt: Implement the URL shortener encode/decode logic

turns.content:
<task>
Implement the URL shortener — encode() should generate a 6-character short code, decode() should return the original URL. Tests are already written.
</task>

1	import pytest
   2	from shortener.codec import URLShortener
   3	
   4	def test_encode_returns_string():
   5	    s = URLShortener()
   6	    code = s.encode('https://example.com')
   7	    assert isinstance(code, str)
   8	    assert len(code) == 6
   9	
  10	def test_decode_returns_original():
  11	    s = URLShortener()
  12	    code = s.encode('https://example.com/page')
  13	    assert s.decode(code) == 'https://example.com/page'
  14	
  15	def test_same_url_same_code():
  16	    s = URLShortener()
  17	    c1 = s.encode('https://test.com')
  18	    c2 = s.encode('https://test.com')
  19	    assert c1 == c2
  20	
  21	def test_different_urls_different_codes():
  22	    s = URLShortener()
  23	    c1 = s.encode('https://a.com')
  24	    c2 = s.encode('https://b.com')
  25	    assert c1 != c2
  26	
  27	def test_decode_unknown():
  28	    s = URLShortener()
  29	    assert s.decode('abcdef') is None

1	class URLShortener:
   2	    def encode(self, url: str) -> str:
   3	        pass
   4	
   5	    def decode(self, code: str) -> str:
   6	        pass

arguments.summary: Read 5 tests: encode returns 6-char string, decode returns original, same URL = same code, different URLs = different codes, unknown code returns None. Codec is a stub. Need hash-based approach with internal mapping.

next_steps.description: Implement URLShortener with hashlib-based 6-char codes and bidirectional mapping
